title: Manually Configure Sentinel Licenses on Linux
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms
Manually Configure Sentinel Licenses on Linux
text: Manually Configure Sentinel Licenses on Linux Normally, the Uniface installer automatically configures the Sentinel license environment on Linux. However, if you ran the installer without root privileges, or you choose the manual configuration option for some other reason, you must manually configure license management. To manually configure licenses, you need to run the Uniface license utility ( ulic ) as root. For more information, see Uniface License Management Utility (ulic) . Run ulic as root: ulic /adm=USYSUNIFACE/adm /prep Copy To activate a cloud-served license, run ulic : ulic /adm=USYSUNIFACE/adm /act EntitlementID For example: ulic /adm=USYSUNIFACE/adm /act 77329e95-d153-4a4b-aa27-a62fadce Copy To activate multiple cloud-served licenses, run ulic with each Entitlement ID as a separate argument: ulic /adm=USYSUNIFACE/adm /act EntitlementID /act EntitlementID For example: ulic /adm=USYSUNIFACE/adm /act 77329e95-d153-4a4b-aa27-a62fadce /act 85972e95-d143-5a5b-aa27-a39fadce Copy To specify an RMS server or license file, set $LICENSE_OPTIONS in the usys.asn file: [SETTINGS] $LICENSE_OPTIONS LM_LICENSE_FILE="a_dir/lservrc" Copy For more information, see $LICENSE_OPTIONS .
